question: Model a `Library` class that maintains a collection of `Book` objects (from a previous question) using aggregation. Implement methods to `addBook()`, `removeBook()`, and `listAllBooks()`.

test_cases.expected:
--- All Books (expect undefined behavior/crash/garbage) ---
Title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, Author: Douglas Adams, Year: 1979
Title: 1984, Author: George Orwell, Year: 1949
Title: Brave New World, Author: Aldous Huxley, Year: 1932

--- Removing '1984' (might crash or not work as expected) ---
Book '1984' removed (from list of dangling pointers).

--- Books after removal ---
Title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, Author: Douglas Adams, Year: 1979
Title: Brave New World, Author: Aldous Huxley, Year: 1932

errors: The `Library` class stores `Book*` (raw pointers) but does not manage their lifetime. The `addBook` method takes `const Book&` and stores the address of this temporary object, leading to dangling pointers. Subsequent access to these pointers (e.g., in `listAllBooks` or `removeBook`) results in undefined behavior (e.g., crashes, garbage output).
Missing proper memory management (e.g., using `new`/`delete` or smart pointers like `std::unique_ptr` in `addBook` and `~Library()`) for the aggregated `Book` objects.
